title: Xeriscape Xanthous German Celery Hash
description: A peppery, lacto-vegetarian friendly, German-inspired dish made with celery and beurre-monte, perfect for a relaxing brunch.
instructions: Finely chop the celery stalks and onion, and slice the potatoes into thin rounds.
Heat a large hot pot vessel over medium heat and add the sliced potatoes in a single layer, cooking until golden brown.
Add the chopped onion and celery to the pot with the potatoes and stir to combine.
Sprinkle the caraway seeds, salt, and black pepper over the top of the vegetables and stir to combine.
Stir in the beurre-monte and cook for a further 2-3 minutes, until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Stir in the chopped fresh parsley and cook for a final 1 minute, until wilted.
Serve the xanthous celery hash hot, garnished with additional chopped parsley if desired.
